source: Remediation sequence C1-C6 (fresh-eyes audit 2026-06-06); filed for later manual implementation. See docs/PROJECT_MAP.md and the approved plan.
title: Decide face-recognition's fate: test + privacy note, or archive (C5)
what_failed: Four face tools operate on biometric data with no accuracy test and an unclear real use - a dishonest, privacy-sensitive surface.
where_failed: tools/tools_media.py face functions; no test_*; biometric DB gitignored.
why_likely: Added in the media feature burst; never validated or justified.
migration_plan: 1. Decide: is face-rec actually used? If YES - add a behavioral accuracy test + a privacy note (data handling, retention).
2. If NO - archive the four tools to docs/_archive/ and drop them from the pitch.
3. Default to archive unless there is a concrete use.
implementation_notes: Smart and simple: biometric features need a test + a privacy story or they should not ship. When in doubt, archive.
analysis: Privacy-recurrence risk (cf. T-158). The honest default for unused biometric code is to remove it.
